Fig. 5: Segmentation results contrast with different models including the SAM optimized model, FastSAM, CrowdSAM, and our DPMO-RPS model. And the robust mask annotations are in the rightmost column.

Fig. 1: Traditional crowd analysis methods primarily analyze based on the coordinates of individual points and the overall density map, but a direct correlation between the two is difficult to establish. Our segmentation mask can establish a determined relationship between the origin coordinates and the density map.
Table 1: Segmentation performance comparison between our model and other models.
Fig. 3: SAM and fine-tuned models performance in different thresholds. Segmentation mae indicates difference between the true amount of people and the number of masks including points.

Fig. 2: Pipeline of our proposed DPMO-RPS framework for crowd instance segmentation, including DPMO segmentation paradigm using NNEC constraint and RPS network trained with GRPO. Supervised by manually modified mask annotations obtained by our DPMO-RPS framework from point annotations, we train the optimized PET model to obtain more accurate predicted points and perform sampling. Then, we train the PSNet with GRPO to select the best point from each group as a final point prompt to assist SAM in segmentation. Finally, with the NNEC constraint, we can achieve effective crowd instance segmentation.
